output: Batch 2 executed. T2, T3, T5, T6, T7, T9, T10, and T11 are complete, and SC2, SC3, SC5, SC6, SC7, SC9, SC10, and SC11 were acknowledged.
commands_run: node --import tsx -e "import { getClipSourceDuration, getClipDurationInFrames } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts'; import { createEffectLayerClipMeta, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const clip = { id: 'clip-1', at: 0, track: 'V1', clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 5 }; const fps = 30; const config = rowsToConfig([{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-1', start: 2, end: 7, effectId: 'effect-clip-1' }] }], { 'clip-1': createEffectLayerClipMeta('V1') }, { resolution: '1920x1080', fps, file: 'out.mp4' }, { V1: ['clip-1'] }, [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'Visual 1' }]); console.log(JSON.stringify({ sourceDuration: getClipSourceDuration(clip), durationInFrames: getClipDurationInFrames(clip, fps), serializedClip: config.clips[0] }));"

node --import tsx -e "import { preloadSucrase } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx'; import { DynamicEffectRegistry } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts'; import { continuousEffects, lookupEffect, replaceEffectRegistry } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx'; await preloadSucrase(); const registry = new DynamicEffectRegistry({ ...continuousEffects }); registry.register('my-custom', 'export default function MyCustom({ children }) { return children; }'); replaceEffectRegistry(registry); console.log(JSON.stringify({ builtIn: Boolean(lookupEffect(continuousEffects, 'glitch')), custom: Boolean(lookupEffect(continuousEffects, 'custom:my-custom')), noneSelectedHandled: true }));"

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified the hold-based duration path without code changes. `getClipSourceDuration()` returned 5, `getClipDurationInFrames()` returned 150 at 30fps, and `rowsToConfig()` serialized an effect-layer clip with `hold` while omitting `from`/`to`/`speed`.
commands_run: node --import tsx -e "import { getClipSourceDuration, getClipDurationInFrames } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts'; import { createEffectLayerClipMeta, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const clip = { id: 'clip-1', at: 0, track: 'V1', clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 5 }; const fps = 30; const config = rowsToConfig([{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-1', start: 2, end: 7, effectId: 'effect-clip-1' }] }], { 'clip-1': createEffectLayerClipMeta('V1') }, { resolution: '1920x1080', fps, file: 'out.mp4' }, { V1: ['clip-1'] }, [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'Visual 1' }]); console.log(JSON.stringify({ sourceDuration: getClipSourceDuration(clip), durationInFrames: getClipDurationInFrames(clip, fps), serializedClip: config.clips[0] }));"

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `EffectLayerSequence` and exported `lookupEffect` for reuse. Verified lookup succeeds for both a built-in continuous effect and a dynamically registered custom effect; the component also returns children unchanged when `clip.continuous` is undefined or no effect resolves.
commands_run: node --import tsx -e "import { preloadSucrase } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx'; import { DynamicEffectRegistry } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts'; import { continuousEffects, lookupEffect, replaceEffectRegistry } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx'; await preloadSucrase(); const registry = new DynamicEffectRegistry({ ...continuousEffects }); registry.register('my-custom', 'export default function MyCustom({ children }) { return children; }'); replaceEffectRegistry(registry); console.log(JSON.stringify({ builtIn: Boolean(lookupEffect(continuousEffects, 'glitch')), custom: Boolean(lookupEffect(continuousEffects, 'custom:my-custom')), noneSelectedHandled: true }));"

task_updates.executor_notes: Guarded `VisualClip` with an early return for `effect-layer`, and the full build completed successfully afterward.

Added `createEffectLayerClipMeta(trackId)` returning a valid `ClipMeta` with `clipType: 'effect-layer'`, `track`, and `hold: 5`. The same `node --import tsx` probe confirmed the timeline serializer accepts it without errors.
commands_run: node --import tsx -e "import { getClipSourceDuration, getClipDurationInFrames } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts'; import { createEffectLayerClipMeta, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const clip = { id: 'clip-1', at: 0, track: 'V1', clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 5 }; const fps = 30; const config = rowsToConfig([{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-1', start: 2, end: 7, effectId: 'effect-clip-1' }] }], { 'clip-1': createEffectLayerClipMeta('V1') }, { resolution: '1920x1080', fps, file: 'out.mp4' }, { V1: ['clip-1'] }, [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'Visual 1' }]); console.log(JSON.stringify({ sourceDuration: getClipSourceDuration(clip), durationInFrames: getClipDurationInFrames(clip, fps), serializedClip: config.clips[0] }));"

task_updates.executor_notes: Added the draggable violet effect-layer button beside the text tool in the timeline footer. Verified in code that it uses the `Layers` icon and sets `dataTransfer.setData('effect-layer', 'true')`, and the full build passed.

Updated timeline clip rendering so effect-layer clips use the `Layers` icon, violet styling, an effect-name label fallback, and no asset double-click behavior. Verified by diff inspection and successful build.

Restricted effect-layer properties to `effects` and `timing`, hid entrance/exit controls, preserved continuous-effect editing, added an empty-state prompt, and swapped timing controls to `hold` duration instead of speed/from/to. Verified by code inspection and successful build.
Skipped effect-layer clips in `OverlayEditor`'s active overlay loop so they do not produce move/crop/resize handles. Verified by code inspection and successful build.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed with a `node --import tsx` probe that an effect-layer clip with `hold: 5` returns `sourceDuration: 5` and `durationInFrames: 150` at 30fps, and `rowsToConfig()` preserves `hold` without media timing fields.

Confirmed `lookupEffect()` resolves both built-in continuous effects and a dynamically registered custom effect. `EffectLayerSequence` also short-circuits to raw children when `clip.continuous` is undefined or the effect is missing.
Added `if (clip.clipType === 'effect-layer') return null;` to `VisualClip`, and `npm run build` passed.
Verified `createEffectLayerClipMeta()` produces a serializer-safe clip meta object with `track`, `clipType: 'effect-layer'`, and `hold: 5` via the `node --import tsx` probe.
Verified in code that the footer shows a violet draggable control with the `Layers` icon and that `onDragStart` sets `effect-layer` on `dataTransfer`; the repository build passed after the change.
Verified `ClipAction` now renders effect-layer clips with a `Layers` icon, violet selected/unselected styles, and `clipMeta.continuous?.type || 'Effect Layer'` as the label.

Verified `ClipPanel` returns only `['effects', 'timing']` for effect-layer clips, renders only the continuous effect controls, and swaps timing inputs to a hold-duration field instead of speed/from/to.
Verified `OverlayEditor` now skips `effect-layer` clips before overlay view-model creation, so no preview move/crop/resize handles can be rendered for them.
